reviews.review_text:
Very happy with the van site: ground covering and hedged.
Facilities were modern, fresh and clean.
Staff took care of us during our extended stay as car needed repairs.
Great extra activities: mini golf, kids playground, pool, camp kitchen, outdoor BBQ and eating areas and we'll equipped games room.
Great place to stay noting its about a 9 minute drive out from the centre. Would recommend!

This is a lovely, beautifully designed, and carefully manicured caravan park! I felt welcome from the minute I walked into the office to check in or even before that when I rang up to make the reservation! And their after hours check in process is seamless!! I just love it when you walk in after a long drive and the heater or aircon is on!!
It's just far enough off the highway that you don't get much highway noise.
Logically set out, neat, tidy, secure.
They have a section for short caravans that need a spot with an ensuite or need to be close to facilities, but you have to reverse in. They also have a section of what they call "11 metre" where there is a bit more space to manoeuver while backing in. This is for longer, dual axle vans, which may have a toilet and shower in them.
No drive-through spots. Every spot either had a slab or matting.
This time I stayed in Cabin 4. 2 bedrooms 3 bunks, queen bed ensuite bathroom. Beautiful, comfy, spacious, decent sized fridge, everything you need. The previous two times I stayed in Settler Cabins. HUGE!! Too big for one person, but just awesome!! Perfectly clean, everything worked. Comfy double or queen bed with a thin doona and summer blankets in the cupboard for every bed and bunks to fit three or four more. Air conditioning in the main living area, but you have to leave the bedroom doors open to get the benefit. And, there is no blind on the front door which means that the early morning light leaks in if you have the bedroom door open. However, they have ceiling fans, but if Mum and Dad want some privacy, they miss out on heat or cooling!! LOL
Very thoughtfully provisioned, it's got everything you need to get started before you go to the shops - bath mat, hand towel, shampoo, soap, tea & coffee, tissues, washing up stuff, a sharp knife (which is really important).
Microwave, iron, and board, toaster & kettle, broom, great aspect with a lovely table out on the veranda at which you could sit and watch the sunset or just enjoy the outdoors.
I'm slightly hesitant to bring my van back here, but I will be back.
I hit the jackpot with #1 Topaz Mining Hut!! Check the pix.
Got the Settlers #4 next check the pix.
Rundown old joint, shower/toilet block wasn't cleaned the 2 days we were there. Site's are poky. Over rated will bypass next time.
always the first priority to choose if travel to bathurst

Spent 3 nights here on a powered site. The sites are very tight to manouver onto, also even though there are lots of signs asking you to seperate your rubbish , none of the bins are marked . The amenities block is big and new , it wasn’t cleaned once during our stay.
Ok caravan sites. Would be testing if park was full to reverse in.

Stayed on a powered caravan site for a week. Ablutions excellent.
Staff are great and very helpful. Good location. Would stay again if in  Bathurst.

Great place to stay with kids for a week.

The good: Good location, nice cabins, Wifi, game room, laundry, mini-golf, good hot water, good reverse cycle aircon, friendly staff.

The bad: Not that close to restaurants.  2/6 laundry machines down, spotty wifi, game room needed maintenance.
Very nice park ,reception lady also. Clean amenities and lots of bins, nice flat sites.

Stayed here a couple of weeks ago… immediately met with an abrasive attitude from the staff.. location was awful, could not put the tent pegs into the ground. When using the amenities we found spoilt, yellow milk sitting in the fridge..no space to park car.. Just an all round bad experience. Never coming here again.

Older style park but quaint and the staff were helpful and nice. The pool was very cold but lots of fun and very clean. The playground was tired but nice and the crazy gold was very cool and a big hit with my young kids. We had a powdered site for our caravan and we got lovely sunsets at night time. There was enough space on slabbed sites and the toilet facilities were large and clean. It does not have a security boom gate but it felt safe and next to lots of handy industrial shops like anaconda and bunnings etc incase you need van/camping essentials:)
Great place for family to stay as we had our caravan and puppy dog
Small park on western end.  Amenities clean and tidy. Not too far to town.  Price with NRMA or equivalent,  Big4 discount reasonable at approx $36-37 per night with P&W.  Would stay here again.
Great park, the ladies in the office are very helpful, the facilities are excellent, only let down by the cleaning of the ablutions.

Stayed here in a powered site. Amenities excellent. Loved the pool especially with the heat, and looked like it had been renovated.  The only issue was the site's were hard to get onto with the narrow road, especially when there were vans opposite. Main road noisy at night.
Great park in a good location good amenities stop and stay

Loved the atmosphere 👌 popular c'van park
Very clean.. friendly..
Great pool area and BBQ

Great little park, well maintained by staff, managers are lovely people and highly helpful. Amenities were clean it would be nice to see the actual park patrons mop the showers out as they should etc and clean up after them selves especially over loading the bins and leaving rubbish around the pool area, not particularly fair the management have to clean that up!

We recently stayed here after the recent rain on the coast to come inland and was very welcoming from the time we checked in till the time
We left the park. Sites were easily accessible be  mindful on bigger rigs and bigger vans as can be a little tight but not impossible, we got into our site with a hybrid van and ranger with cabins in front no worries.

Good value for $$ would recommend for a stop especially if you love mount panorama and blue mountains in close proximity. Is close to some good shops despite what other reviews say about location local fruit and vegetable down the road was good for saving some $$ and great local produce.
Enjoyed our stay in our caravan, his facilities and pool.

An established park with rustic cabins and tight caravan sites.
Parking is not easy as access is from roadways only. Needs a redesign to accommodate current sized vans and tow vehicles. Dump point requires a stop at the pub for the return trip!
No views from caravan site as stated there were. Toilets needed cleaning. Everything felt dirty and unkept. Playground equipment dirty. Gardens full of weeds.

This is the second NRMA site that was dog friendly,and those dogs barked then and are going off now trying to have some peace on my break, and the underside of the toilet seats were brown ,the kids play area is also very dirty
Caravan sites are gravel with no barrier between caravans. Internal roads narrow. Located on main highway. Issues with water while we were staying.
Old cabin, the room sliding door broken, window mosquito net broken, wifi not working.

Our powered grass site? Gravel and a concrete slab.

Toilets and amenities? Far from majority of the sites.

Security? Just an amenities code, no boom gate or secured access what-so-ever.

All sites are tiny and back onto a fence or building so the kids are left to play in the road if they wish to stay near the camp-site or go to a sandpit, average pool or rocky / grassy mini golf area.... there's no grassed area for them to play as all the sites are rock, dirt and gravel.

For what we paid we definitely aren't staying there again.
I recommend booking directly on their website or over the phone as reservation made at booking.com has brought so much confusion during check-in. Otherwise overall experience was great!
Very tidy dog friendly caravan park

This NRMA Holiday resort has much to offer, including a swimming pool with a water slide, mini golf, and a playground. The reception staff were really friendly, and the rooms were very good quality, with a big bathroom and comfortable beds. It makes as an awesome holiday place, and is very kid friendly!

The park is a little far out of town. The show grounds were are first choice but we couldn't get in due to events being held there. Turns out the caravan park was cheaper than the showgrounds.  We have bikes so rode into town and around the river where we found a lovely cafe.
The site pads are roomy crushed gravel sourounding the pads a bit dusty on windy days. Ablutions clean and usable. Laundry 4 dollars a load, plenty of clothes lines.
The water had a musky smell when washing and taste when drinking, so the ablutions and laundry had that same smell. We drank the water and survived. There was a pool, mini golf, playground and camp kitchen.
Pretty chilled place, toilet block was clean showers were good. They even let us watch the end of race in the games room on the big screen
Friendly.. clean .. comfortable.. great location.. everything you need.  👍👍
Dirty not real clean  full of grubs it was an aweful stay never again

The park is quite run down and lots of areas are just a little neglected.
Pool was good for the kids.
Most disappointing part was that most of the Caravan spots are on gravel or surrounded by gravel.
It's a big effort to have a holiday and when you get here not a blade of grass in sight. Seems like a lazy option just to dump gravel for a caravan spot.
We have stayed at lots of NRMA parks and this is probably the most run down we have been to.
Staff were very good and friendly.

Perfect location for me .. arrived from a long day of driving to friendly reception and free upgrade.. and so very much appreciated to find the aircon on and room lovely and cool ... how very thoughtful..thankyou so much.. I left you a little thankyou gift .. sorry I didn't have a pen to write a note . Rooms are beautiful and clean and bed so comfortable.. and has everything you need. Highly recommend this lovely quiet spot . Thanks again.
Usual NRMA quality park. Great service. Neat and clean

We had a great experience here and the service was next level amazing! We had car trouble and didn’t get into the park until much later than intended. We also ended up needing an extra night because our car wasn’t ready for us to leave. The staff were fantastic, supportive and understanding. They made the whole process easy and hassle free. I would highly recommend staying here. We didn’t really use many of the facilities but the putt-putt was fun and different and the ensuite on the ensuite site was brilliant and well kept.

We just got back from a long weekend in Bathurst and we stayed at Bathurst Panorama Holiday Park. I came on here to share our feedback because we had such a great time here and I am really shocked to see all the 1 star recent reviews. I dont think its fair that most of them are from the same family e.g husband and wife leaving 2 separate reviews of the exact same nature.

Anyway, back to my experience; we stayed in the Panorama room which is directly opposite the pool. Myself and my four kids. The staff were fantastic.  The cabin was far beyond our expectations.  It was huge, spotless, well equipped and we all loved it. We were only there 2 nights and were out most of the time exploring Bathurst so didnt get to explore many of the amenities but overall I would give this place a 5/5 and we will be back again very soon for another stay. Special thanks to the Lovely Manager who welcomed us after hours and was very hard working. I saw him around scrubbing bbqs, cleaning the pool etc
. See you next time!
Ellena

Disappointed. Travelling from interstate, you rely on the information they provide on their web site. “Activities, board games, pool table, cinema” they forgot to add in the fine print, of which there is clearly a lot of.
Should read something like this
“Activities are not run by us,” “we don’t actually offer any board games” “the pool table is ripped and doesn’t work” “we only turn the cinema on during the weekend.” “The water slide doesn’t work.”

I feel completely misled. I think you can forgive one or two mistakes but this is on another level. I would think this type of deception is exactly why we have the ACL 2010, ACCC and the like.

School holidays stay 30/9/25 to 3/10/25 Room was nice and clean. All toiletries provided and dishwashing liquids - see reception if you need more. After hours check in was seamless. Received text updates while staying at the park which was handy.  It was advertised that board games were available at reception however reception stated they knew nothing about this. (Pop down to the friendly op shop in Bathurst for a board game instead. Service was lovely.) The games room - all games require payment and the main one we were excited for (pool table) the table was terribly ripped and unusable. It was advertised that they facilitate school holiday activities, the only advertised activities were activities ran independently in town. The pool slide was out of service. Many of the washing machines and driers out of order in the laundry. Only 2 face washers provided in a cabin for 5 people. Also the cabin only had 4 indoor chairs, the neighbouring cabin had 5. Ceiling fan was filthy and only had 1 smoke alarm in kids room, nothing in the double room but some wires that had been pulled out. Big stain on the carpet. We enquired about the indoor cinema for our kids, they stated this is only used on weekends. We were very dissatisfied with our stay as we chose this particular park based on the facilities that were advertised and not supplied.

We needed somewhere to stay while our car was being fixed. These guys were amazing and made us feel very welcome. They went above and beyond for us.
Would definitely stay again. We got a powered site for our Caravan. Sites are nice and flat and we were very comfortable.
Amenities were clean. The putt putt golf was a wonderful feature, we didn't get have a play this time round but hope to next time we stay.
Thank you guys for making us feel very welcomed.
Recommend Bathurst Mount Panarama Holiday Park.

The staff were lovely and really friendly, however the positive feedback stops there. I've stayed at many holiday parks and this was the worst we've ever stayed in. The reason people stay in places like this is because they have kids, and these places have amazing amenities and activities for families. This place had no jumping pillow, no daily activities, the pool was dirty and the slide was broken, and the playground equipment was tiny and something straight out of 1980. The games room had a pool table that was ripped, had splintered wood, and no ball or pool cues, the arcade games were broken faded and antiques. I haven't even got to the room. The cabin was tiny, and there was a significant amount of mould on the bedroom ceiling, and the couch had ripped fabric. The only feature of interest for the kids was mini golf, but that's not enough to save this place. It's clear the owners let things wear out or break, and they just don't replace, or update features. Shame.

Recently stayed here wanting a weekend a way with the family. Less
Then impressed, jumping pillow hasn’t been there for over 6 years the service lady tells me. I mentioned it’s in the brochure in the room and always advertised online and they said they are getting on to it. Games room most the stuff is broken and filthy. Swimming pool not working and in the warmer weather.

Cabin overall was good location great just the cabins are outdated and need some maintenance. Hot and cold taps would be good if they had labels. Booked for 2 nights stayed for 1.

Mini golf was the best thing about the park!

Quiet park. We were close to park facilities. Park is a little tired and the amenities block could have been cleaner. No soap in the soap dispenser for our entire stay and not the cleanest amenities we have experienced. Plenty to do for kids.
Caravaning

Very outdated and they’ve missed the mark with the ‘mining’ theme however quite affordable for families. Pool was closed for unknown reasons and would have loved a dip with our family but overall was reasonable.

I guess it was okay for the price you paid for a powered site. It was a bit smelly and dingy. I think they missed the mark on the old mining theme… came across a little bit worn out. The place might need a bit of an upgrade. To keep up with its competitors..

Stayed here recently for 2 nights on ensuite site. Staff were very friendly and moved us to different site on our request.

Park needs some upgrades but overall I think it was good and value for money.

Enuite camp site was good and private. Shower was good.

Mini golf course was good. To cold for pool so can't comment on that.

Would stay here again.
Great place

Spent 4 nights at this caravan park on an ensuite site.
The park is quite picturesque and the ensuite is modern and clean, with instant hot water.
Reception was welcoming and friendly.
Enjoyed our stay at this caravan park.

Quite a way from the city centre, not a coffee shop to be found.  The campsite is in need of a lot of TLC, the only redeeming feature being the amenities block which has obviously been renovated. The place is an OHS nightmare, with irregular steps with dodgy handrails, potholed roads and not a roadside kerb in tact. Definitely not friendly for anyone with a disability.  Not the standard I’d expect from an NRMA caravan park!
toilets and showers are not cleaned everyday

We stayed here in our camper van so I can’t vouch for the rooms.
The location is a little out of town but easy to get to on the main road.
The service at reception was ok.
The amenities are top notch! Super clean and modern.
The site is clean and tidy. Love the hedges everywhere. It made our site feel private and green. Lots of birds around which was lovely.
They had no problem with 3 little fur kids - which was great!
Amenities is very essential for a caravan park. It is new and clean. It has washing machine and dryer. Mini golf for children and playground. The caravan park is quite safe and Very family friendly.

The studio cabin itself was great. Convenient location, clean and very affordable. The only negative is the bed was absolutely horrific, so very soft and lumpy - to say it was uncomfortable is an understatement. In saying that, what may be uncomfortable for some may be perfectly suitable for others. I would book again and just hope the beds in the other cabins are more comfortable - my back is pretty ruined.
Large park
NRMA caravan Park. Renovated amenities and artificial grass on some sites make this an excellent park.
Good caravan park

A great park plenty to do and see, the caravan sites are clean with concrete, astro or mat and plenty of room to set yourself up. We had along stay due to work commitments at Mt Piper Power station the booking staff considered this when giving our site we shared our area with a permanent residents and it worked well. Our long days at work and the peace and quiet for a good night's sleep thank you for your consideration of our situation. The amenities where brilliant running hot water anytime of the day plenty of showers and toilets for the busy holiday periods clean and well maintained, for me the best part was the paper towel to dry your hands on its the little things that count. Friendly staff at the office and on grounds weather permitting a beautiful pool area to relax and cool of, camp kitchen, games room with cinema. Not to far from the town centre,cinema, eating out or shopping. Some great road trips to Hill End, Sofala, Mudgee, Blackheath and the Blue Mountains of course. Anyhow two very happy campers thank you to all at NRMA Holiday Park Bathurst.
Mark & Michelle

I am in disbelief at the way I was spoken too. I am a local who lives on a nearby farm. After explaining in a very friendly manner, after floods that I had no water for the past 3 days, due to a faulty water tank, and asking could I please pay to use the showers, I was totally taken back by the unkind, rude and abrupt response. I had them on speaker, just expecting they would help out, and my daughter and I could not believe the rudeness, like I was trying to scam a free shower!  I was truely saddening and I told them that the lack of community help and support astounded me. Bathurst community usually help each other out. This company needs to understand it's not just their tourists they should be kind to, and Bathurst locals are not parasites! Lucky there are much kinder and more understanding hospitality owners about, who helped me and my daughter out.
Narrow roads inside the park, sites are stone or road base with shade cloth awning areas. Amenities and Laundry are clean and in good order.
No WiFi connection only place it would connect is reception staff not interested in fixing problem

Came with a camper trailer, sites are that tight its very hard to back your trailer into it without having to drive onto opposite person's site. Screaming babies all night, kids running around screaming till 11pm, camp-site uneven. First and last time we'll come here.
Went there for camping with families and friends. The facilities were clean but a bit out of dated.
The price of an unpowered site almost doubled to $61 per night due to the public holidays and a racing event at the race track. Sites were sloping and uneven, ridiculous prices. Wont be returning

We were put in a pet friendly cabin, we did not have a pet with us, and it was in poor condition! Thought it was awful. Ceiling fan didn't work. Toilet cistern crooked, looked like it could fall off. Damp rotten floor from leaking shower felt like it could collapse. Sofa all ripped up. Scuffed up furniture which is so uncomfortable you can't sit on it. A light in the bedroom doesn't work. No WIFI. The place really needs to be gutted. It wasn't what I would call cheap either! We're long term NRMA members and are disappointed NRMA would have such poor cabins. We will certainly never stay here again!

Ensuite sites don't supply towels! Different from other Big4 parks.  Shower not great.... wouldn't suit properly and water pressure really varies.  I'd love to post something positive about this place but I'm a bit sleep deprived from the school group who stayed up late in the van park!!! Won't be staying at this location again.

Friendly & welcoming Staff.
Super Clean amenities, Laundry & BBQ areas for all to use.
Impressive Pool with Water Slide for the kids.
Loved Bathurst and the race track. Stayed 3 days as lots to see. Loved the toilets near the unpowered sites. Very convenient. Showers were clean. Handy camp kitchen. Enjoyed a game of putt putt
Adequate,  toilets showers clean. Bit overpriced with lots of free camping nearby.

We stayed five nights on an ensuite powered site.
Check in by lovely staff easy.
Our site (98) was a good size and level. We were able to park our car off the road.
Ensuite was a good size, clean, bath mat, hand towel, face cloth and some toiletries supplied. Water was hot and good pressure.
I used the laundry and it was clean. Only $4 a wash. Plenty of clothes lines .
BBQs clean and hot. Camp kitchen looked a little tired and not as clean as it could have been. No hot water to wash up.
Recreation area and pool lovely.
Garbage bins in a central area and plenty of them.
A great park. Highly recommended.
Booked this park to explor Bathurst and surrounding areas.  Clean amenities and tidy well laid out park. Eazy access and close to major retail hub great stay overall.
The park is a bit run down

Children should be supervised in amenities at all times, the amenities were disgusting, floors not washed once while we were there, toilets had faeces on back of doors and walls, toilets not being flushed, sinks not cleaned left with scum rings, the showers had no shower curtains and no barrier for water, everything got wet
Definitely not to big 4 standards, difficult to back van due to the elevated guttering
Mainly camping
Great accomadation. Good lacation.
Nice park pool bbq etc. But on suites sites for parking your van very tight no room for your car .
A1.Five star 🌟

Stayed over Christmas for a week and this place is fantastic I stayed in my jayco on a ensuite site and the facilities are so clean the laundry room is clean and well equipped with washing machines and dryer’s including a ironing board and iron great camp kitchen and a bbq area and for the kids they have a swing set pool with slide rec room with arcade games and putt putt golf
The price is very reasonable the staff are excellent very friendly
Bad services treated badly and I called two days to check with my cabin and said that I’m getting a fossckier but got a different cabin when I checked in

If you have difficulty reversing you van then this park may not be for you.  Very tight when everyone parks their cars in at night.  We stayed one night with ensuite site.  Ensuite was lovely, park is nice.  Couldn't find a bin anywhere near us so took our rubbish with us.

At $70/night I felt it was expensive compared to other places we have stayed.

We recently enjoyed a wonderful family vacation at NRMA Bathurst and were thoroughly impressed. The park itself was impeccably maintained, with a range of excellent facilities for all ages. Our cabin was spotlessly clean and comfortable, providing a cozy base for our adventures. The highlight for the kids was undoubtedly the fantastic swimming pool – it was the perfect place to cool off after a day of exploring. The staff were friendly and helpful throughout our stay, making us feel welcome from the moment we arrived. We highly recommend NRMA Bathurst to families seeking a fun and relaxing holiday experience.

Stayed  two nights in a caravan site.  Park was small but nice and tidy and the site we were on, was extra large and out of the way, which was handy as we have two dogs with us.  The amenities were really clean and did plenty of walks with the dogs.  Would recommend
Was told 10% off BUT when we got there we only got 5% off

Great location and cabins were very clean. The cabin was perfect for my partner and I, great size, clean bathroom and room and decent sized fridge for the night. Staff were lovely and we enjoyed our stay here
Really lovely park. Quite modern amenities, very clean. We arrived on a 32 degree day and the pool was such a lovely relief. The staff were super friendly and great location on the outskirts of town.
This is one nice place to stop over for the night or for a couple of days
Highly recommend the pet friendly cabins. Comfortable and all you need for a short stay. Great facilities and staff.
Great park

What a wonderful park and ammenities were very clean and tidy. High pressure shower was amazing and appreciated the upgrade to an ensuite site. |Loved the veggie garden for people to help them selves . Happy and cheerful staff too Thank you would highly recommend

Never stay there charged 420 for 7hrs instead of 2 nights that was confimed on booking and even chaged the booking details after we booked online when they seen the colour of our skin then insulted the traditional custodians the manger is claiming to be Aboriginal now because I call her and the house keep out on the racism and they steal you things if you leve anything behind the call police and claim we didn't leave when asked and cause a fuss so I've asked the police for her proof of this and guess what the manger and housekeeper have nothing shame shame never stay there save your money

If you're looking for swimming, there's a pool. There's also a small mini golf course. We stayed 4 nights over a weekend, on a powered camper site., and it was busy. Toilets and showers clean and not too busy. The roads and pitches are sandy, not grassed which we didn't like due to the sand coming into the camper! Just up the road to the right as you exit, there are kangaroos.
Nice park ,everything you need . A little way out but can recommend.

Quite surprised to find out how dirty the bathroom and kitchen by the time we stayed there. There was a young woman who constantly drove her car to the camp kitchen and left the dust that spread to the sitting area and she parked right at the spot where “No parking day and night” sign, didn’t seem bothered her at all.

The pool was okay, the mini golf was great. The lady at the check in counter was nice and friendly.
Caravan park is great but some sites are hard to get in with even a small van

Great caravan park very clean and friendly staff.
Amenities very clean. Highly recommend. And it’s pet friendly.we decided to stay an extra night.
Best toilet block in four thousand kilometres. Only sad the pool was closed

Clean, tidy, easy access, Pet Friendly without putting you in the scummiest place like alot of places do. |So clean and the bed is really soft and pristine. Lady on reception was absolutely lovely and we booked a second night as soon as we saw the room. Easy walking over the road to Petbarn if you’ve forgotten anything for your munchkins and takeaways close and handy. |Laundry onsite and clothesline. Highly recommended!

Amazing. Very friendly, very clean & modern ammenities. Pet friendly. Only negative is the lack of grass for dogs to sit, sniff & do their business. But in the scheme of things that’s a minor negative.
Thank you Kyra for looking after us.
Great caravan park for NRMA members

Great value park well maintained and beautiful modern clean amenities. Mini golf, small playground and pool open in the warmer months. Big games room next to the camp kitchen and another separate under cover bbq. I stayed on a powered site, choice of slab, Astro turf and green mat. The staff were great they put me near the amenities which I appreciated cos I don’t have a bathroom in my tiny caravan. Also ensuite sites available. Variety of lovely cabins as well. Easy so access strait off the highway but there’s very little traffic noise. Only complaint was the hot water in the shower wasn’t hot just warm unfortunately. Short easy drive into town and to the famous mount Panarama racing circuit. I will definitely be back

Unmaintained powered sites. We had a huge muddy ditch on ours, that we had to jump over. Others were similar. When speaking to the office we were told we requested near amenities. What about all the empty sites opposite the amenities?  I was told they are for motorhomes....we have a motorhome!!! Then I was told head office inspected earlier in the week & they were aware of the poor sites, yet they continued to put us and others in them.
Facilities are great. Site's very uneven. Ours had a huge muddy hole making it hard to level van

Not a bad little park. Had an ensuite site. They are small but that is a minor inconvenience. The games room was a little tired.
Staff were lovely.
Nice easy to get to,close to town.
nice cosy holiday house, we stayed at Fossilic room type, balcony had nice city view.
Pretty good caravan park. I sleep in my car when possible. I arrived after dark and after reception was closed, but got served thru an after hours number, and the receptionist arrived in 10 minutes.

Toilets and shower facilities were relatively new which was nice and in summer the pool and mini golf would be good for kids. It’s a shame the jumping pillow has been taken away and the games room was pretty bad. Lots of broken toys and tv. No access to projector, pool cue, air hockey etc. it was a rainy weekend when we were there so the kids could really feel the lack of pillow and games room in terms of trying to keep them
Entertained. Hopefully they look to fix that soon.
Good size powered sites clean facilities

We stayed here on the way through to the Hunter valley but didn't imagine that the ensuite sites would be as small as they were and we had to unhitch our caravan even though we were only there for one night.Wouldnt have had room to park the car down next to the caravan otherwise we wouldn't have been able to open the caravan door so had to park in front of the van instead.Also ensuite is on the opposite side of the van.Good thing was the laundry was only $4 to wash and $4 to dry although we had to put the dryer on a second time as the first drying cycle didn't dry the clothes properly.Also having a Garbage truck coming into the park at 5.20am in the morning disrupting guests is not real pleasent.Unfortunately due to what I have mentioned we would not stay here again.
Cabin suitable for 4
Budget for NRMA but could be getting a much better place for the same $$? airBB or something, we just thought this was going to be better for the kids but a bit disappointing.

After-hours check in with a key lock box accessed by a code was great when our travel got delayed. Communication with the hotel was easy via text.

Stayed in a cabin, bed was comfy, and it was super clean. Decor/fit out is clearly from the 90s but that's not an issue what it made me see is just how well it's been kept in the last 30 years!

The shower had a small tub which was perfect for my toddlers' night time bath. Many places we stay at on the road don't have a tub so I was very pleased to see that!

There was a playground, mini golf, a pool (with a slide!) and an arcade room which we didn't get to enjoy because we had to get back on the road but we all decided we'd definitely be coming back for a proper stay in the warmer months!
Very quiet park, very clean and tidy  modern amenities block I would recommend this park
It wasn't good at all , very run down cabins
Great value. Amenities excellent, flat sites. Staff very friendly and helpful. Looks like an excellent park for kids, lots of play grounds and activities. Good base to explore Bathurst and area.

We stayed in a two bedroom cabin for two nights whilst our daughter son in law and granddaughters camped.|The cabin was compact and clean..plenty of towels but the bunk bed mattresses were vinyl covered and quite hard.|The park itself was very disappointing..they advertise a giant jumping outdoor pillow for the kids..well that was non existent..the kids playground very underwhelming and the sandpit was really dirty..we wouldn’t let the girls play in it..|The recreation room was awful and very very dirty and unkept.|We thought we’d give the putt putt a go but they wanted to charge us $8 per adult and $4 a child..ridiculous it just wasn’t worth it..not a great experience for us..so glad we only stayed two nights..
Great  caravan  park. Very friendly staff and clean facilities.  Kids enjoyed  the mini golf course. Even had a swim.  Big sites and well maintained.

Good park. Quality facilities.  Quiet ish location. Actually cheaper than other parks if you're a member of nrma
I enjoyed our stay
Sunset views from the hill are fantastic..
Site is dated but clean. Nice pool. Games room needs an upgrade.
I have stayed at this park several times now and the service from the team is by far the best service I have had from a big 4. I would recommend for anyone traveling to Bathurst to stay here
We had a lovely stay, just an overnighter but will be back next time we are down this way. Clean and modern amenities and good camp kitchens. Friendly staff.

Great place to stay - could do with a bit of a facelift overall, but otherwise, very comfortable. Very accommodating, as online all cabins were booked. Had to find a place at short notice, when I called, they were able to squeeze me in. No road noise where I was, and very comfortable. Easy access to all of Bathurst. Thanks for the pleasant stay, and will definitely be back.

Not the parks fault but there was lots of traffic noise that went all night.
Amenities were clean and well maintained.
We stayed in unpowered site
Great place to stay 👌 👍
As with all NRMA sites very good facilities.

Pets are welcome!

Camped for 4 days. Sites were really clean and well looked after and so were all of the amenities.  Staff were really accommodating and friendly.

Such a wonderful place to stay. Very friendly people. Down to earth

Cabin was very nice / clean

Thank you

See you next January
Love this holiday park,highly recommend
Great park, clean amenities.
Visited our family who were staying here. They loved the park, pool and putt putt BUT we had a bbq....the fridge was disgusting, piles of mouldy rubbish 🤢

What a wonderful place to stay!
We stayed in one of their cabins for the week, and quite frankly, didn’t want to leave. The cabins are clean and modern, and the park is beautifully maintained. They have a pool with a slide, a wading pool for the little ones,  and an awesome games room with a pool table, air hockey, and a number of game machines to enjoy. They also have a large theatre screen, with a movie played most nights which was my favourite.
There is a large ammenities block with great bathroom/shower facilities as well as a laundry area with washers, dryers and a number of clothes lines. Plenty of bbq’s and sheltered eating areas, with some great views of the country side. The staff are very friendly and welcoming, Keiran was super helpful and ensured we had everything we needed to enjoy our stay.
Very nice park. Good for families and alot of activities for the kids.

Good value for money for families with young kids. Safe, clean, close to town & the adventure park/playground & museums.
A bistro or a cafe would have been a worthy addition.

Great park with a lovely pool area. We stayed in the Panorama Cabin which was perfect for a family. It’s flies to the main highway so gets a bit of road noise at night from all the trucks passing through.

Fantastic caravan park!! We stay in a lot of caravan parks travelling to tennis tournaments & this caravan park is the best! It's close to everything & they have put hedges up between powered sites which gives you space & privacy.
位置很好 旁边有家很便宜的加油站 整个公园都很干净 前台特别热情 很推荐！
Great. Should have taken my pillow.

The cabin was the epitomy of a real cabin. The miners Cabin was absolutely Charming, while being sterile enough inside to be kept clean. Such a relief to see some design and aesthetics in a caravan park. And all it really takes is a nicely pitched roof. That adds the cute factor and distracts from the ATCO boxes that usually pass as CABINS in Australia. Even the inside had a cute pitched ceiling. So unexpected.
First time staying here, great location
Ensuite site, spotless. Close to town, mini golf, pool, good size camp kitchen with TV. Staff super friendly.

Nice stay, we stayed in one of the prospector cabins for a night, it was clean but getting a little old, not that I’m complaining as it was nice and cozy with a Tv and dvd, air conditioner, kettle, fridge and heater, overall it was nice and quiet, also had it own car spot, the only complaint was one of the taps in the bathroom was hard to open and was dripping, the soap holder in the shower was also missing

We had a Studio cabin. When we checked in we were given the key to our Studio but another person was already in it!  we were both surprised but all was sorted out and we were given another Studio. |The bed was comfortable and our Studio was in a quiet location. |There were some communal clothes lines.   Our advice is the user dries clothes at their own risk on the line. We washed 2 pairs of socks and hung them on the clothes line and there were no other clothes on that line except our socks. When we went to bring them in, only one pair was on the line. Somehow the other pair, which were new, was no where to be seen. We reported it to Management and were told that they might have been blown away by the wind!  It was not a windy day!!  We were also told that they never had any issue of clothes being stolen before. It must have just mysteriously disappeared.  We were glad we didn't hang any other clothes on the line.

UPDATE: Unfortunately Bathurst NRMA management and staff encourage discrimination. A simple call to senior management now has this situation resolved. You could have done the same thing. It is your job as management after all.

Blacklisted by Max Monning for having an Assistance Animal for Post Traumatic Stress Disorder. Disgusting discrimination NRMA. You're a disgrace to Australia NRMA.

RESPONSE:
Response from the owner 3 days ago
Hi E C Thank you for taking the time to review, unfortunately as a result in being blacked listed from NRMA parks we were unable to ACCEPT your booking. We wish you the best, Keiran Bathurst NRMA Holiday Park Manager

We had booked a cabin for us and one for some friends and the Receptionist decided to put an extra $40 in for the friends then charged us $19. When we tried to ask why we were quoted an amount and extra upon arrival, they told us that the rates had change d and asked that we not be aggressive. Both receptionists said that our friends were not welcome at the caravan park and tried on two occasions to take out the money as they canceled in bookings. They managed to take out on the third time and our $19 was refunded a few days later. The Receptionist were rude, lacking customer service and I wouldn’t recommend anyone going there. The staff need customer service training asap.
We have only just got here so we are not sure. The studio where we are staying in very compact but comfortable and clean.

We tried to book 5 powered sites together this year in December but was told that they cannot guarantee that went we all arrive we would be together and could easily be scattered because the if other caravanners come before you they would be given a site due to the size of their vans especially if the they cannot back their van in the spaces that is allocated to them in a different site. NRMA park is not a cheap park and if they cannot guarantee you the site that you have booked perhaps because of the position etc or in our case so our family can be together this is so ridiculous and stupid. We will not bother to come to this park now and No meeting from time to time in the camp kitchen as suggested to us is not an option.
Very good Place

Great park, the best mini golf I've found to date. Facilities clean and well maintained. The site itself is probably due for a reno, the old green ground mat has seen better days and the gravel around it not so compacted anymore. Don't forget your torch for the midnight run to the loo, it's a bit dark. Just off the highway easy access to town but we didn't hear the road noise, will definitely return.
A good park with a mix of sites it has a big play ground fo kids we were given an onsuit site all in all a good park
A lovely place on the outskirts of Bathurst.

Clean toilet block, good BBQ facilities and pool was refreshing 👍
Mini golf ⛳️ was also a great activity to do at the park.
So nice I would stay for 20 years
Excellent  &  polite  service a real pleasure  to chat  on arrival  will be sure to stay again
Awesome place to stay, staff very friendly and helpful. Facilities are great and clean. Has the atmosphere you want after a long drive.

We booked a powered camp sire with ensuing, it was absolutely beautiful. The staff working there were so friendly and helpful, made the stay even more worthwhile. Had heaps for the kids to do including free mini golf. Would def come back and highly recommend.

Our family were here 03/07 - 06-07. We stayed in the Ruby cabin. The cabin was spotlessly clean, with crisp, thick sheets on all the beds, two TVs, a large fridge, oven, microwave, plenty of blankets, a quiet air-con. The grounds are kept clean, as well as the bbq areas and plenty of things to keep the kids busy, like a playground, putt putt golf, etc. We give the office staff 10/10 for their efficiency, friendliness and helpfulness. Nothing was too much trouble.
Great place to stay. Our cabin was well equipped and the bed was comfortable. Great family park. Lots of things to do for the children.

Better during the summer (rather than the winter, when you will need to port your own heating elements to be properly warm). However, the staff are friendly and attentive. If you don't know how to activate the stove or if there is a power outage, they will render a visit and resolve it. I enjoyed staying in the One-Bedroom Miner's Hut close to the entrance. I adore this holiday park. You will just need to conduct your own cleaning prior to departure. In my opinion, this is an act of discipline which most Australians need (because they are spoilt). It is not too much trouble for hardworking Asians like myself.
Woke up to - 4. Good spot
